context_units.diff_patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+from calculator import add, divide
+
+
+def test_add():
+    add(2, 3)
+    assert True
+
+
+def test_divide_by_zero():
+    try:
+        divide(10, 0)
+    except Exception:
+        pass

raw_diff_chunks.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+from calculator import add, divide
+
+
+def test_add():
+    add(2, 3)
+    assert True
+
+
+def test_divide_by_zero():
+    try:
+        divide(10, 0)
+    except Exception:
+        pass

expected_findings.title: Test asserts True instead of the result
expected_findings.rationale: test_add calls add(2, 3) but asserts True, so it never verifies the return value and would pass even if add were broken.

expected_findings.title: Exception swallowed without assertion
expected_findings.rationale: test_divide_by_zero catches all exceptions and passes; it never asserts that the expected error is raised, so it would pass even if no error occurred.
